summary_one_line: A short input of small low-valued myanmar_category bytes that drives the syllable state machine into the broken-cluster transition (case 8).

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A short byte buffer (~24-44 bytes) of mostly small low-valued bytes (0x00, 0x0c, 0x20, 0xfe) repeated in a structured pattern so info[p].myanmar_category() walks the Ragel myanmar syllable machine into the trans_action == 8 broken-cluster transition at line 502.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Naive's plain havoc byte mutators (BytesCopy/BytesInsert/ByteInc/ByteAdd) produce the small structured low-byte buffers (e.g. '10 10 00 00 0c 0c 0c 00' repeated 0c/20/fe units) that map onto the myanmar_category ranges; all 10 naive trials resolve and the winner hits case 8 with hitcount 38 (W: case 8 T=21).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Cmplog's inputs are dominated by repeated CMP-derived literals (runs of f9, magic ASCII like 'hasc', 'csah', 'SHOT'/'OTS' fragments) that satisfy table-header comparisons elsewhere but never form the small-category state-machine sequence; the loser never reaches case 8 (L: case 8 T=0, blocked 8/10).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The winner is enabled by naive's undirected havoc byte mutation (BytesCopy/BytesInsert/ByteInc/ByteAdd), which freely explores the small low-byte structured space that drives the myanmar_category state machine. Cmplog's input-to-state CMP-operand substitution actively rewrites bytes toward intercepted comparison operands (table magic / ASCII tokens), pulling inputs AWAY from the small-category pattern, so the comparison-substitution technique is a net liability here and the pure-havoc naive arm wins. The winning mechanism is therefore havoc-only byte mutation.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is naive>cmplog with delta I2S; naive resolves 10/10 and cmplog blocks 8/10.
The winner naive resolves all trials while the loser cmplog mostly blocks.
The blocker is the broken-cluster transition case 8 in the Ragel myanmar syllable machine.
The state-machine transition is selected by info[p].myanmar_category(), i.e. the per-byte category drives which case fires.
Winner seeds are short buffers of small low-valued repeated bytes produced by plain havoc mutators.
Winner seeds were discovered by naive's havoc byte mutators, not by any I2S mutator.
Loser cmplog seeds carry CMP-derived literal/token runs (f9 runs, ASCII fragments) and TokenReplace, not the small-category pattern.
The winner reaches case 8 (T=21) while the loser never does (T=0), confirming the divergence is at the broken-cluster transition.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 502  T=21 F=107  T=0 F=157  case 8:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Cmplog's distinguishing technique is I2SRandReplace, which substitutes intercepted CMP operands into the input.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: Naive's mutators are pure havoc/token byte mutators with no I2S, matching the winner-seed provenance.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Claimed mechanism is havoc-only (winner is naive, which has no I2S), so lineage I2S-floor verification does not apply. The decisive pair is naive>cmplog: cmplog OWNS I2SRandReplace yet LOSES, so I2S cannot be the winning mechanism; the winner's resolving seeds (Seed 1 id=a1568bfee9e19ce4 mutation_op=BytesCopyMutator, Seed 2 BytesInsert/BytesExpand, Seeds 4-5 ByteInc/ByteAdd/ByteNeg) are all plain naive havoc byte mutations with no dash rows and no CMP substitution.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with the same small-category state-machine gate where cmplog reaches case 8 at >= the naive rate, showing I2S substitution does not actually hinder reaching the broken-cluster transition.

weakest_evidence_point: The claim that cmplog's I2S substitution actively pulls inputs away from the small-category pattern is inferred from the seed-byte contrast (f9/ASCII runs vs small low bytes) rather than directly observed in cmplog lineage.
